title: CI hygiene: pin all GitHub Actions to reviewed immutable commit SHAs
objective: Replace all 11 tag-pinned action references (9 in .github/workflows/ci.yml, 2 in .github/workflows/generate-lockfile.yml) with immutable commit SHAs resolved live from the GitHub API for the latest release of each major tag (actions/checkout@v4, actions/setup-node@v4, actions/setup-python@v5, actions/upload-artifact@v4), each with a trailing tag comment in the same style secret-scan.yml already uses; NOTHING else changes - no formatting, ruff, dependency, or functional edits
business_reason: Owner directive 2026-07-17: SHA-pinning must land before any repo/CI secret (B-001/B-002 credentials pending); secret-scan.yml already demonstrates the standard; G5 M0-T005-R1 and M2-T001 F1 recorded this debt
inputs: .github/workflows/ci.yml (9 tag-pinned refs: checkout@v4 x4, setup-node@v4 x2, setup-python@v5 x2, upload-artifact@v4 x1)
GitHub API tag->SHA resolution via gh api (live verification mandatory; never from model memory)
outputs: All 11 refs pinned to the immutable commit SHA of the latest release in the SAME major version line (no major upgrades), each with a '# vX.Y.Z' trailing comment matching the secret-scan.yml style
Producer report with the exact gh api commands + outputs proving each tag->SHA mapping, and a diff summary proving ONLY 'uses:' lines changed
forbidden_paths: everything else - including .github/workflows/secret-scan.yml (already pinned), .github/scripts/**, apps/**, services/**, packages/**, docs/**
acceptance_scenarios: S1 primary: every 'uses:' line in ci.yml and generate-lockfile.yml references a 40-hex commit SHA with a trailing '# vX.Y.Z' comment; zero '@vN' tag refs remain in those two files
S2 verification: each SHA is proven equal to the commit the official tag points to via recorded gh api output (dereferenced annotated tags handled correctly - tag object vs commit object)
S3 same-line rule: each pinned version is the latest release of the SAME major already in use (checkout v4.x, setup-node v4.x, setup-python v5.x, upload-artifact v4.x); no major bumps
S4 no-collateral: git diff shows changes ONLY on 'uses:' lines (plus the producer report file); YAML parses; job/step structure byte-identical otherwise
S5 regression: full CI green on the branch after pinning (all 6 jobs) - proves the pinned SHAs actually resolve and run
S6 consistency: identical action+version pairs use identical SHAs everywhere (no mixed pins of the same action)
risks: SHA must come from the official actions/<name> repo tag (watch for annotated-tag dereference: gh api the tag ref, then dereference object.sha to the COMMIT sha)
A wrong SHA bricks all CI - S5 branch CI run is the proof gate before merge
Scope creep is prohibited by owner directive: no formatting/ruff/dependency/functional changes may ride along
execution_location: owner PC for file edits + gh api resolution (KB-scale, disk now 7.77 GB free - above floor); CI validation in GitHub Actions on the task branch; cleanup = worktree removal after acceptance
